page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server instance featuring 288 vCPUs across 144 physical Intel Xeon 6985P-C cores. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture platform includes 504 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is equipped with 1080 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 3.75 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance features bundled local storage consisting of six NVMe SSDs totaling 16,242 GB. In benchmark evaluations, the hardware demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, exceeding 1,086 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 428 GB/sec for uncached large-block writes. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top decile, with Redis achieving 401.21 million operations per second.

While the platform excels in high-throughput tasks like static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and prompt processing for medium-sized language models, it exhibits relatively lower performance in single-threaded text generation for smaller models. Lacking discrete GPU accelerators, it relies on CPU-based matrix instructions for AI workloads. This configuration is highly suitable for large-scale databases, high-performance computing, software compilation, and intensive data-processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server configured with 288 vCPUs and 1080 GB of DDR5 RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 platform features 144 physical cores and a 504 MB L3 cache. It includes 16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives, but lacks discrete GPU acceleration. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong software compilation capabilities. However, text generation performance for smaller language models is relatively weak. This instance is optimized for data-intensive workloads, large-scale databases, and high-throughput enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1080 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 16.2 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server with 288 vCPUs, 1080 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-core bare-metal platform featuring massive local NVMe storage and DDR5 memory for demanding database and data-processing workloads.
bullet_points: 288 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors at 4.2 GHz
1080 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
16,242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data pipelines
